title: Add image-based language games for community use
notes: Suggestion #3 from admin export (submitted by mannyrayner on 2026-05-09), refined by planning discussion on 2026-05-09 and update suggestion #2 submitted on 2026-05-15. Add image-based language games, with Kok Kaper Indigenous community work as the immediate use case and a target of having the initial version stable before Sophie's next visit in early June 2026. Use the community picture dictionary as the central curated resource for both picture glossing and games. The 2026-05-15 update identifies a concrete fast path: use the successfully migrated legacy C-LARA project `50 words in Kok Kaper` (one approved word/image pair per page) as the seed material for a Kok Kaper picture dictionary, preserving most project structure while adding dictionary metadata, entry readiness state, and community ownership. Then adapt/generalize the existing word-based flashcard infrastructure so it can create image-based flashcards in both directions: (1) show an image and ask the learner to choose the matching word from alternatives; (2) show a word and ask the learner to choose the matching image from alternatives. Start by clarifying the plan in docs/roadmap/picture-dictionaries.md and docs/roadmap/exercises.md, then implement (a) legacy-project-to-picture-dictionary import/registration for projects like `50 words in Kok Kaper`, and (b) picture-dictionary-backed flashcard generation/play. Before implementation, agree the user-level workflow with Sophie. The first implementation should keep routine operations in the Community Organiser picture-dictionary interface, support generating images just for newly added/missing-image words, use approved/game-ready dictionary entries for flashcards, choose distractors from the same dictionary with optional AI ranking/filtering, and provide simple learner-facing play plus a lightweight review/feedback path for bad images, wrong words, or bad distractors. Update suggestion #3 submitted by mannyrayner on 2026-05-15 records concrete progress: the legacy C-LARA project `50 words in Kok Kaper` has now been successfully imported and installed as the first version of the Kok Kaper community picture dictionary. Treat the seed-import/registration part of the fast path as implemented; remaining work should focus on validating/curating dictionary entry readiness, using the imported dictionary for picture glossing where appropriate, and building the two image-game flashcard directions from approved entries.
